free_text: Systematics of Fragment Mass and Energy Distributions
For Proton-Induced Fission of U-233,U-235 and U-238.
Proton beam currents in range 100-300 nA.
Targets made by vacuum evaporation of uranium oxides
onto backings of nickel about 70 mug/cm**2 thick or
carbon about 20 mug/cm**2 thick. Fissile deposits
20-50 microg/cm**2 thick; isotopic purity in all
cases > 98%.
Two silicon surface-barrier detectors of the
heavy-ion type were used to detect the fission
fragments.
Not corrected for neutron emission from fragments.
Data-point reader uncertainty.
No information given on data uncertainties.
Scanned from figures in reference by CAJAD.
Data points not given for 233U and 238U measurements.
CAJAD + VM
SD. In Subent 003 REACTION was code
updated according to last EXFOR rules.
Fission yield normalized to 200%.
SD. In REACTION code:
SF6=AKE changed to SF6=KE; SF7=FF changed to SF7=LH+HF
